question:
Will Casper Ruud win the 2025 Rolex Paris Masters Men’s Singles Tournament

Description: This polymarket will resolve to the player who wins the 2025 Rolex Paris Masters Men’s Singles Tournament.

The market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed player wins the 2025 Rolex Paris Masters Men’s Singles Tournament. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this player to win the tournament per the official rules of the 2025 Rolex Paris Masters, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

evidences.title: The seedings order for the 2025 ATP Rolex Paris Masters is now known | Puntodebreak.com
evidences.content: To become champion, one will need to win five matches starting as a seed, and six matches if not enjoying that status. It is important to note that there will be 16 seeds, unlike the 32 in the rest of the Masters 1000 events of the year, except for Monte Carlo. The draw for the ATP Rolex Paris Masters 2025 will take place this Friday, October 24th, with the exact time yet to be determined (last year it was at 7:30 PM), leaving the weekend for the qualifiers and commencing the main draw on Monday, October 27th. ### Potential Matchups Between Seeded Players Round by Round Round of 16 Seeds 1-4 face seeds 13-16 Seeds 5-8 face seeds 9-12 Quarterfinals Seeds 1-4 face seeds 5-8 Semifinals Seeds 1 and 2 face 3 and 4 ### Order of Seedings for the ATP Rolex Paris Masters 2025 [...] Strong emotions are looming between October 27th and November 2nd, with the ATP Rolex Paris Masters 2025 taking place, the last Masters 1000 event of the year. Not only will all the participants for the ATP Finals 2025 be decided, but the world's top two players, Carlos Alcaraz and Jannik Sinner, will once again come together at the same event. We analyze their potential opponents round by round based on the o...

evidences.title: Casper Ruud Targets 2025 ATP Finals: "All or Nothing at This Point"
evidences.content: ### Race to the ATP Finals As the long and arduous ATP calendar arrives at the indoor hard-court European swing for the autumn months, the finish line at the end of another taxing season moves into focus. With that comes one final opportunity for a late rankings push ahead of the off-season. For the ATP's leading players, that also comes with the added impetus of potentially sealing a spot in the season-ending ATP Finals. Currently 12th in the rankings, the odds are against Ruud clinching one of the last few places in Turin. He will have to perform impeccably for the remainder of the year to have a chance of sneaking into one of the coveted top eight qualification positions. evidences.title: Will bigger truly be better at the Rolex Paris Masters?
evidences.content: Tennis.com Watch now ATP Paris, France # Will bigger truly be better at the Rolex Paris Masters? At the tournament's new home, Carlos Alcaraz and Jannik Sinner are in the same draw for the first time since the US Open, while Felix Auger-Aliassime and Casper Ruud make 11th-hour bids for Turin. By Steve Tignor NANTERRE, FRANCE - OCTOBER 26: Carlos Alcaraz of Spain plays a backhand while training in preparation for the Rolex Paris Masters 2025 on October 26, 2025 in Nanterre, France. (Photo by Franco Arland/Getty Images) © 2025 Franco Arland Carlos Alcaraz and Jannik Sinner haven’t wasted any time sweeping through the ATP’s most important events. Between them, they’ve won all four majors, the ATP Finals, the Davis Cup, and eight of the nine Masters 1000s. Neither has turned 25 yet. [...] Can either, or both, of them turn that around in 2025? The vibes seem right this time. Alcaraz is coming here as the world No. 1, and has won 18 straight non-Laver Cup matches. Sinner will arrive after a title in Vienna on Sunday, and 21 straight wins on indoor hard courts. > View this post on Instagram > > A post shared by Tennis (@tennischannel) Alcaraz starts against either Cameron Norrie or Seb...

evidences.content: Finally, Roland Garros and US Open runners-up Neal Skupski and Joe Salisbury complete the line-up of confirmed doubles stars set to contest the title at the Accor Arena. It's been a memorable year for the British pair, who have reached five ATP finals this year. That said, they're still on the hunt for their first piece of silverware and will be motivated to get their breakthrough in Paris. ## Who are the top seeds at the Rolex Paris Masters 2025? 1. Carlos Alcaraz (ESP) 2. Jannik Sinner (ITA) 3. Alexander Zverev (GER) 4. Novak Djokovic (SRB) 5. Taylor Fritz (USA) ## Who are the Rolex Paris Masters reigning champions? Men’s singles: Alexander Zverev (GER) Men’s doubles: Santiago Gonzalez (MEX) & Edouard Roger-Vasselin (FRA) ## Past British winners at the Rolex Paris Masters? [...] ## Past British winners at the Rolex Paris Masters? The Rolex Paris Masters has seen four British tennis players be crowned champion across the singles and doubles draws. Rolling it back to 1998 where Greg Rusedski became the first British singles champion in Paris, having defeated then world No.1 Pete Sampras 6-4, 7-6(4), 6-3. Oslo-born Ruud, 26, holds 13 ATP singles titles and has been a stand-out contender among the next generation of players, compiling excellent results on clay and hard court surfaces. evidences.title: French Open 2025: Casper Ruud criticises 'rat race' ATP points system after second-round exit - BBC Sport
evidences.content: The top-ranked ATP players are required to participate in eight of the nine mandatory Masters 1,000 events each year, with Monte Carlo the only exception. Ruud missed Monte Carlo - the first big clay-court event of the season - but he played at the Italian Open in Rome after winning his first ATP Masters title in Madrid. The world number eight also feared that he would miss out on defending ranking points, which are used to decide where a player is seeded and whether or not they can gain direct entry into a tournament. "You feel like you lose a lot if you don't show up and play, both economically, point-wise, ranking-wise and opportunity-wise," Ruud added. "It's a questionable system because on one hand you don't want to show up injured, and you maybe give the spot to another one." [...] "As we know, it's a hectic clay season, and I decided to kind of push through it doing some anti-inflammatory pills and painkillers to try to get rid of it, which has helped to a certain degree but not enough. "I will have some more time now to let it heal and rest for a long time." Ruud said it is difficult to take time off on the ATP Tour to allow an injury to heal. "It's kind of like a rat ra...

evidences.title: Rolex Paris Masters | Get the most out of your visit
evidences.content: Rolex Paris Masters: Your Complete Guide to the Prestigious Tennis Tournament Rolex Paris Masters: Your Complete Guide to the Prestigious Tennis Tournament Last updated: Jun 17, 2025 · 5 min read # Rolex Paris Masters The Rolex Paris Masters stands as one of tennis’s most prestigious events, drawing the world’s elite players to the French capital each year. This ATP Masters 1000 tournament represents the final Masters event of the tennis season, often serving as a crucial battleground where rankings are cemented and year-end championships qualifying spots are secured. Whether you’re a dedicated tennis enthusiast or planning your first visit to this world-class sporting event, this guide provides everything you need to know about attending the Rolex Paris Masters. [...] The tournament follows a traditional Masters 1000 format, beginning with qualifying rounds, followed by the main draw featuring 56 players competing in single-elimination matches. The early rounds typically run from Monday through Thursday, with quarter-finals on Friday, semi-finals on Saturday, and the championship match on Sunday afternoon. Daily sessions often begin around 11:00 AM, with evening sessions starti...

evidences.title: About Casper
evidences.content: ### Scored by human raters Each scenario is evaluated by a different member of our trained group of raters, ensuring a fair assessment of your skills. Our raters undergo rigorous training, so they can assess your responses accurately. Learn more about how Casper tests are rated. ### Scores sent to programs 2-3 weeks after you take your test, your Casper score is automatically sent to the programs you’ve selected for distribution. Programs will update your application status within their application portals. Learn how to prepare for Casper ## Reserving your Casper test [...] ## How does Casper work? ### 2 sections, 11 scenarios Casper has 2 main sections: video responses and typed responses. Each section will present you with word-based and video-based scenarios. ### Video responses (4 scenarios): Each scenario will ask you two open-ended questions. You will have one minute to record a video for each response. ### Typed responses (7 scenarios): For each scenario, you will type answers to two open-ended questions, which will be presented on the same page. You will have 3.5 minutes total to respond to both questions. ### 65-85 minutes, with breaks Casper typically takes 65-85 minut...

evidences.title: Rolex Paris Masters 2025: New arena and player entry list

Final draw sizes and prize-money figures are to be published closer to the tournament. As the last Masters 1000 stop before the ATP Finals, Paris is expected to be important for players chasing qualification for Turin, and the new arena could affect indoor hard-court playing conditions.
